functions.label: DateRangePicker
description: A date range picker component that accepts natural language and date math input.

functions.label: useDateRangePickerContext
description:
Hook to access the DateRangePicker context.
Must be used within a `DateRangePickerProvider`.

functions.label: useDateRangePickerPanelNavigation
description:
Hook to access panel navigation inside the date range picker dialog.
Must be used within a `DateRangePickerPanelNavigationProvider`.

interfaces.label: DateRangePickerContextValue
description: Public context value exposed to consumers via `useDateRangePickerContext`.

children.label: text
description: Current input text

children.label: isInvalid
description: Whether the current input is invalid

children.label: setText
description: Update the input text

children.label: applyRange
description:
Apply the current text (or an explicit range) as the selected time range.
When called with a `TimeRangeBounds`, sets text to that range; otherwise applies current text.
Pass an optional `textOverride` to control the input value instead of generating it from the bounds.
Calls parent `onChange` and exits editing mode.

children.label: startDate
description: Start as Date object

children.label: endDate
description: End as Date object

children.label: value
description: Text representation of the time range

children.label: isInvalid
description: Whether the time range is invalid

interfaces.label: DateRangePickerPanelConfig
description: Configuration for a consumer-provided panel inside the date range picker dialog.

children.label: id
description: Unique panel identifier, used for navigation

children.label: title
description: Title shown in panel header and navigation button

children.label: icon
description: Icon type passed to `EuiIcon` for the panel navigation item

children.label: component
description:
Panel component to render. Must be a component reference (not an element).
Rendered inside the provider tree so it can use
`useDateRangePickerContext()` and `useDateRangePickerPanelNavigation()`.

interfaces.label: DateRangePickerPanelNavigationContextValue
description: Context value exposed via `useDateRangePickerPanelNavigation()`.

children.label: activePanelId
description: Id of the currently visible panel

children.label: panelDescriptors
description: Descriptors for all registered panels

children.label: navigateTo
description: Push a panel onto the history stack and navigate to it

children.label: goBack
description: Pop back to the previous panel and navigate back

children.label: canGoBack
description: Whether there is a previous panel to go back to

children.label: 'data-test-subj'
description: Test subject selector added to the outermost container element.

children.label: css
description: Emotion CSS styles added to the outermost container element.

children.label: className
description: Passed to the main container.

children.label: isLoading
description: Shows a loading spinner inside the form control.

children.label: value
description:
Text representation of the time range (controlled).
When provided, the component is controlled and `value` is the external source of truth.

children.label: defaultValue
description: Initial text representation of the time range (uncontrolled, ignored when `value` is provided).

children.label: onChange
description: Callback for when the time changes

children.label: dateFormat
description: Custom format for displaying (and parsing?) dates

children.label: isInvalid
description: Show invalid state

children.label: disabled
description: Disables the control and time window buttons.

children.label: onInputChange
description: Called when the editing input text changes.

children.label: width
description:
Horizontal sizing behavior.
- `'auto'` — shrinks to fit content (inline-flex).
- `'restricted'` — sets the width of the control to a fixed value.
- `'full'` — stretches to 100% of the parent (flex).

children.label: compressed
description: Reduce input height and padding

children.label: collapsed
description:
When `true`, the idle-state control hides its text label and only shows
the short-duration badge.

children.label: showTimeWindowButtons
description:
Show time window buttons (previous, zoom out, zoom in, next) beside the control.
Pass `true` for defaults, or a config object for fine-grained control.

children.label: panels
description:
Additional panels rendered inside the dialog popover.
Each panel is navigatable via `useDateRangePickerPanelNavigation().navigateTo(id)`.

children.label: presets
description: Predefined time range options shown in the Presets section.

children.label: recent
description: Recently used time ranges shown in the Recent section.

children.label: onPresetSave
description: Called when the user wants to save the current input time range as a preset.

children.label: onPresetDelete
description: Called when the user wants to delete a saved preset.

children.label: calendarOptions
description: Calendar-specific options (e.g. first day of week).

children.label: settings
description: Current picker settings (e.g. rounding, refresh).

children.label: onSettingsChange
description: Called when the user changes a setting in the settings panel.

children.label: timeZone
description:
A valid time zone name, from the IANA database, e.g. "America/Los_Angeles".
This is only informational, it won't affect how dates are handled.

interfaces.label: DateRangePickerSettings
description: User-facing settings exposed by the date range picker settings panel.

children.label: roundRelativeTime
description:
When true, relative time ranges round to the nearest full unit
(e.g. minute, hour, day).

interfaces.label: TimeWindowButtonsConfig
description: Configuration for time window buttons.

children.label: zoomFactor
description:
How much the time window is increased/decreased when zooming.
A number between 0 and 1, or a percentage string e.g. "25%".

children.label: showShiftArrows
description: Show buttons for shifting the time window forward and backward.

children.label: showZoomOut
description: Show the "zoom out" button.

children.label: showZoomIn
description: Show the "zoom in" button.
